name: Container Cafe
description:
Container Cafe — Start Your Food Business for Less

SAMAN container cafe installed and operational at a street location in Bangalore. Starting at Rs 8,50,000, this is a fully functional food business — no construction, no lease deposit, ready in 15 days.

A container cafe is the fastest way to open a food business in India — no construction timeline, no landlord deposit, no 3-year lease commitment. Starting at Rs 8,50,000, fully kitchen-ready and delivered to your location in 15 days.

SAMAN designs and manufactures container cafes from our facilities in Bangalore and Greater Noida. Every unit leaves our factory fitted with your kitchen layout, ventilation, electrical, and branding-ready exterior — not as a bare steel box.

What a Container Cafe Costs — and What That Buys You

Opening a traditional restaurant in Bangalore or Delhi NCR means signing a lease at Rs 60,000 to Rs 1,50,000 per month, paying 6 to 10 months as a security deposit, and spending Rs 8 to Rs 25 lakhs on interior construction before you serve a single customer. That is Rs 15 to Rs 40 lakhs committed before your business earns its first rupee.

A SAMAN container cafe starts at Rs 8,50,000. No deposit. No construction. No 3-year lock-in. The unit arrives factory-fitted, ready to plug in and open.

What that investment covers: the container structure, kitchen fitout including exhaust provision, food-grade internal surfaces, electrical with commercial-rated wiring, insulation, one door and window configuration of your choice, and exterior finish ready for your branding.

What it does not cover: kitchen equipment (commercial burners, refrigerators, coffee machines), furniture, signage, and FSSAI registration — these are owner-supplied and owner-registered, because they are specific to your food concept and business name.

What We Build Into Your Kitchen Before Dispatch

The most common question food entrepreneurs ask us: can I actually run a real kitchen in this?

Every SAMAN container cafe is designed with FSSAI fixed-premise kitchen requirements in mind — not as a generic steel box you convert later.

SAMAN container cafe kitchen shell — stainless steel cooking wall lining, exhaust hood position, LPG connection point, and commercial-rated electrical panel fitted at the factory before dispatch. Owner supplies cooking equipment.

Standard kitchen provision on every unit:

  • Exhaust hood position and duct opening — sized for commercial kitchen exhaust fans
  • LPG connection point with pressure-rated inlet fitting
  • Food-grade stainless steel lining on cooking wall surfaces
  • Stainless steel sink mounting with drainage provision
  • 240V commercial-rated electrical with MCB panel — separate circuits for cooking zone, lighting, and AC provision
  • 50mm PUF insulation throughout — maintains stable interior temperature regardless of outdoor conditions
  • Ventilation opening positioned per FSSAI airflow direction requirements — clean to contaminated zone flow

Custom requirements — additional windows for a serving counter, pass-through window for takeaway, modular expansion joining to a second unit — are confirmed at the design stage and built in before dispatch. We do not modify after delivery.

For buyers who need a full kitchen with seating, serving counter, and back-of-house separation, our container restaurant configuration covers that layout in detail.

Match Your Food Concept to the Right Container Format

Your food concept determines your container size and configuration — not the other way around.

Single-product coffee or beverage counter: A 10×8 ft unit with a walk-up service window. Seats 0–4 at a counter. Ideal for a branded coffee kiosk or juice bar at a mall entrance, highway stop, or corporate campus. See our container coffee shop for this configuration.

Fast food or casual dining: A 20×10 ft unit with dine-in seating for 8–12 and a separate kitchen zone. Can operate standalone or connect to a second unit for expanded seating. This is the most popular format for first-time food entrepreneurs.

Full restaurant or food court stall: A 40×10 ft unit or two 20ft units connected — giving you a kitchen, counter, and dining area under one roof. Our modular container cafe covers multi-unit connected configurations.

Two SAMAN 20-ft container units connected to form a modular restaurant with kitchen, counter, and dine-in seating for 20–30 covers. Delivered and installed across Delhi NCR and Bangalore in 7–21 working days.

Pop-up, events, or seasonal concept: A mobile configuration on a flat-bed trailer. Drives to your location, opens for the event, drives back. No permanent site needed. See our pop-up restaurant format for this use case.

Mobile food truck replacement: A container on a trailer chassis — larger than a food truck, with full commercial kitchen capacity. Our food truck container page covers this format.

From Order Confirmation to First Customer — 15 Days

  • Days 1–2: Design confirmation — you approve the layout, kitchen configuration, window and door placement, and exterior colour.
  • Days 3–12: Fabrication at our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility — kitchen surfaces fitted, electrical installed, exhaust provision built, exterior finished.
  • Days 13–14: Quality check and dispatch — unit loaded and transported to your site.
  • Day 15: Delivery and placement — unit placed at your location, electrical connected, and handed over.

SAMAN container cafe fabrication at our Bangalore manufacturing facility. Kitchen wall panels, electrical, and exhaust provision are fitted at the factory — Days 3 to 12 of the 15-day timeline. Every unit is quality-checked before dispatch.

You can begin FSSAI documentation from Day 1 — the kitchen layout drawing from us supports your application. The unit's permanent electrical installation and fixed placement qualify it as a fixed food establishment, which means you apply for FSSAI State License (Form B), not a mobile vendor registration.

The Business Advantage a Lease Can Never Give You

Every food business carries location risk. You research foot traffic, sign a lease, build out the space — and then discover the location does not perform. In a conventional restaurant, that means absorbing 6 months of deposit and years of lease liability.

SAMAN container cafe being relocated using a standard hydraulic crane. If a location underperforms, the entire unit lifts, loads, and moves to a new site in 2–3 days — no lease breakage, no construction cost.

A container cafe changes this calculation entirely. If your location does not perform, you relocate. The cost to lift and move a container cafe to a new site is a one-time logistics expense — not a lease breakage penalty.

This is why container cafes work particularly well as a first food business in India: you can test a location, prove your concept, build your customer base, and scale — without betting your entire investment on a single location decision.

For buyers exploring a multi-location or resort-based hospitality concept, our container hotel page covers how container units can be deployed across a property.

Licenses for a Container Cafe in India — What Applies

FSSAI State License (Form B): Required if your expected annual turnover exceeds Rs 12 lakhs. A container cafe with permanent electrical installation and a fixed placement address qualifies as a fixed food establishment — not a mobile vendor. You apply as a restaurant operator, not a food truck. FSSAI inspection uses the kitchen layout drawing, which we provide with your order.

Trade License: Issued by your local municipal corporation. Required for all commercial food operations. Apply after confirming your placement address.

GST Registration: Mandatory if annual turnover exceeds Rs 20 lakhs. For most food entrepreneurs expecting steady business, register at setup.

Fire NOC: Required for any premise using LPG or high-heat equipment. Your fire department inspects for extinguisher placement, exhaust system, and ventilation — all built into the SAMAN container cafe design.

We do not handle licensing on your behalf — but we provide the documentation (kitchen layout, electrical specification, structural details) that your license applications require.

Container Cafe Range — Find Your Format

The 14 subpages in this cluster cover every food business format a container cafe can serve. The right starting point depends on your concept, your location type, and whether you need to move.

Fixed location coffee and beverage: container coffee shop Fast casual or dine-in: container restaurant, shipping container restaurant, shipping container cafe Multi-unit or expanded: modular container cafe Mobile and events: mobile cafe, mobile container cafe, mobile restaurant, pop-up restaurants, food truck container, portable cafe container Hospitality and resort: container hotel, shipping container hotel, restaurant food containers

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a container cafe cost in India and what is included?

A SAMAN container cafe starts at Rs 8,50,000 for a 20×10 ft unit. This includes the container structure, kitchen fitout with exhaust provision, food-grade wall surfaces, LPG connection point, stainless steel sink position, commercial-rated electrical with MCB panel, 50mm PUF insulation, and exterior finish. Kitchen equipment, furniture, signage, and FSSAI registration are owner-supplied.

How long does it take to get a container cafe operational after ordering?

From order confirmation to delivery at your site takes 15 days — 2 days design confirmation, 10 days fabrication at our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility, and 3 days quality check, dispatch, and installation. FSSAI documentation can be started in parallel from Day 1 using the kitchen layout drawing we provide.

Does a container cafe need an FSSAI license like a restaurant or like a food truck?

A container cafe with permanent electrical installation and a fixed placement address is classified as a fixed food establishment under FSSAI rules — not a mobile vendor. You apply for FSSAI State License (Form B) as a restaurant operator. Mobile vendor or food truck classification applies only to units that move between locations without a fixed address.

Can a container cafe support a full commercial kitchen including LPG cooking?

Yes. Every SAMAN container cafe includes an LPG connection point, exhaust hood provision, food-grade stainless steel cooking wall surfaces, and a commercial-rated electrical circuit separate from lighting and AC. The ventilation design follows FSSAI airflow requirements for fixed food establishments. You supply your own commercial burners, extraction fan, and cooking equipment.

Can the container cafe be relocated if the first location does not work out?

Yes. Relocation requires a flat-bed transport and a crane or hydraulic lift at the new site. The container is lifted, loaded, transported, and placed with no structural modification required. Most relocations complete within 2–3 days including transport — significantly less expensive than lease breakage and refit costs of a conventional restaurant.

What sizes are available and how many customers can a container cafe serve?

SAMAN container cafes are available in 10ft, 20ft, and 40ft lengths. A 10ft unit suits a single-product kiosk serving 20–40 customers per hour with no seating. A 20ft unit supports a full kitchen and 8–12 dine-in covers. A 40ft unit or two connected 20ft units supports 20–30 covers. Custom multi-unit food court configurations are available on request.

Ready to plan your container cafe?

Tell us your food concept, preferred size, and location city. We will come back with a design recommendation, kitchen layout, and full quote within 24 hours — no commitment needed at this stage.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
